title: tillaea drummondii var. bolanderi
intro: Tillaea drummondii var. bolanderi, also known as Bolander's tillaea, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Tillaea drummondii plant, a flowering plant native to the Pacific coast of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for wound healing and soothing skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Bolander's tillaea are attributed to its high concentration of terpenes and flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair. Bolander's tillaea can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve for topical and internal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications and effects on human health.
